 {"id":"gt-4u5z","title":"Refinery as Worktree: Local MR Integration","description":"Move refinery from separate clone to git worktree. Polecats stop pushing to origin - refinery sees their branches locally. MRs become wisps (ephemeral). Only main gets pushed after merge.

## Goals
- Origin stays clean (only main + beads-sync branches)
- No orphaned polecat branches ever
- Simpler mental model for MR coordination
- Faster integration (no network for local MR ops)

## Trade-offs
- Machine crash = redo pending work (acceptable, beads track state)
- Future federation needs different approach (bundles or integration branch)

## Components Affected
- Rig initialization (refinery setup)
- Spawn/polecat workflow (remove origin push)
- Refinery manager (local branch access)
- Documentation (architecture, workflows)
- Molecule templates (remove push instructions)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:24:56.517669-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:24:56.517669-08:00"}

## Changes
- internal/rig/init.go or similar - change refinery setup
- Create worktree at <rig>/refinery/ with branch 'refinery'
- Remove clone logic for refinery
- Update any path assumptions

## Considerations
- Refinery worktree should track main (or its own branch?)
- May need to handle existing rigs (migration path)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:25.927013-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:25.927013-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.1","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:25.92745-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-4u5z.2","title":"Remove origin push from polecat workflow","description":"Polecats should no longer push their branches to origin.

## Changes
- internal/cmd/done.go - remove 'git push origin HEAD'
- internal/cmd/mq_submit.go - remove branch push
- Molecule templates - remove push instructions
- Any hooks that push polecat branches

## Verification
- Polecat completes work without pushing
- Branch stays local
- Refinery can still see the branch (via shared .git)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:27.079456-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:27.079456-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:27.081989-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:43.547484-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-j87","title":"Design: Work flow simulation and validation","description":"Validate GGT designs through simulation before implementation.

## Validation Approaches

### 1. Dry-Run Simulation (Recommended First)
Mayor walks through scenarios mentally/on paper:
- "If polecat Toast signals done with dirty git state, what happens?"
- "If Witness context fills mid-verification, what state is lost?"
- "If two polecats try to close same issue, what happens?"

Create beads for any gaps discovered.

### 2. Real Work in gastown-py
Use Python Gas Town to stress-test assumptions:
- Run actual batch work on test repos
- Observe edge cases in practice
- Document issues found

### 3. Edge Case Analysis
Systematic review of failure modes:
- Agent crashes mid-operation
- Network failures during sync
- Concurrent access to shared state
- Context limits hit at bad times

## Key Scenarios to Validate

- [ ] Witness session cycling (state preservation)
- [ ] Polecat decommission with dirty state
- [ ] Merge conflicts in queue
- [ ] Beads sync conflicts between workers
- [ ] Escalation path (stuck worker -> Mayor)
- [ ] Cross-rig communication
- [ ] Federation mail routing (future)

## Success Criteria

- No data loss scenarios identified
- Clear recovery paths for all failure modes
- Edge cases either handled or documented as limitations
- Design improves as model cognition improves

## Output

For each scenario validated:
1. Document in relevant bead if issue found
2. Create new beads for missing functionality
3. Update architecture.md if design changes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:24:11.251841-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:25:49.858717-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-jpt","title":"Town-level beads: Real DB for coordination mail","description":"Implement Option A from mail redesign: Town gets real beads DB for coordination.

## Background

Mail is now Beads. But currently:
- Town .beads/redirect points to rig beads
- mayor/mail/ has legacy JSONL files
- Cross-rig coordination has no clear home

## Design

Town beads = coordination, cross-rig mail, mayor inbox, handoffs
Rig beads = project issues, work items

Matches HOP hierarchy: platform > project > worker

## Structure

~/gt/
  .beads/           # REAL beads DB (prefix: gm-)
  mayor/
    town.json
    state.json      # NO mail/ directory
  gastown/
    .beads/         # Rig beads (prefix: ga-)

## Tasks

1. Delete ~/gt/.beads/redirect
2. Run bd init --prefix gm at ~/gt/ (town beads)
3. Delete ~/gt/mayor/mail/ directory
4. Update gt mail to use beads not JSONL
5. Add mail fields (thread_id, reply_to, msg_type)
6. Update gt prime for two-tier model
7. Update docs/architecture.md

## Addressing

- mayor/ -> town beads
- rig/agent -> rig beads
- Cross-rig -> town beads","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T19:09:55.855955-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.032558-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.032558-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-kjnt","title":"Polecat Mood Plugin (standard)","description":"Standard witness plugin molecule that assesses polecat emotional state.

## Molecule Definition

```json
{
  "id": "mol-polecat-mood",
  "title": "Assess mood for {{polecat_name}}",
  "description": "Analyze captured output and determine emotional state.\n\nVars:\n- {{polecat_name}} - The polecat to assess\n- {{captured_output}} - Recent tmux capture\n\nOutput: gt polecat mood {{polecat_name}} <emoji>",
  "labels": ["template", "plugin", "witness", "tier:haiku"],
  "issue_type": "task"
}
```

## Mood Emojis

```
😺 working     Active tool calls, making progress
😸 productive  Completing tasks, tests passing
🐱 idle        Waiting at prompt, no recent activity
😼 confident   Self-reviewing, about to submit
😿 struggling  Repeated errors, test failures
🙀 stuck       No progress for 10+ min
😽 done        Work complete, requesting shutdown
😾 blocked     Explicitly waiting on dependency
```

## Bonding

During witness patrol plugin-run step:

```bash
bd mol bond mol-polecat-mood $PATROL_WISP \
  --ref mood-{{polecat_name}} \
  --var polecat_name=ace \
  --var captured_output="$TMUX_CAPTURE"
```

## Installation

```bash
bd mol install mol-polecat-mood  # From Mol Mall
```

## Customization

Fork the molecule and modify description to change assessment criteria or add custom moods. Install your fork to override.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:17:12.841134-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T05:07:39.495061-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-kjnt","depends_on_id":"gt-u818","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:17:20.444775-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-kmn","title":"Batch Work System: Integration branch, merge queue, landing","description":"## Overview

This epic tracks batch work coordination in GGT. Key insight: **work is a stream, not discrete swarms**.

There are no "swarm IDs" - the epic IS the grouping, the merge queue IS the coordination. "Swarming an epic" is colloquial for spawning multiple workers on its children.

## Architecture

- **Epics** group related work
- **Issues** are individual tasks  
- **Dependencies** encode ordering (multi-wave emerges automatically)
- **Merge queue** coordinates completed work
- **Workers** process issues independently

See Key Decision #11 in docs/architecture.md: "Work is a Stream (No Swarm IDs)"

## What This Epic Covers

1. Integration branch management for batch merges
2. Refinery semantic merge processing
3. Witness landing protocol (cleanup)
4. Report generation (epic-based, not swarm-ID-based)
5. Git safety auditing
6. Work reconciliation

## What Was Removed

- Swarm IDs (sw-1, sw-2, etc.)
- Per-swarm directories (.gastown/swarms/<id>/)
- Swarm manifest/state/events files
- swarm-started/swarm-landed beads

These were redundant - beads already provides hierarchy, status, dependencies, and history.

## References

- Architecture: docs/architecture.md (Key Decision #11, Multi-Wave Work Processing)
- PGT: swarm/manager.py, swarm/landing.py (behavioral reference, but ignore swarm ID patterns)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:08:15.339127-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:25:33.208338-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-kmn.1","title":"Swarm package: Swarm, SwarmTask, SwarmState types","description":"Define core swarm types.

## Types (in `internal/swarm/`)

```go
// SwarmState represents swarm lifecycle
type SwarmState string
const (
    SwarmCreated   SwarmState = "created"
    SwarmActive    SwarmState = "active"
    SwarmMerging   SwarmState = "merging"
    SwarmLanded    SwarmState = "landed"
    SwarmFailed    SwarmState = "failed"
    SwarmCancelled SwarmState = "cancelled"
)

// Swarm references a beads epic that tracks swarm work
type Swarm struct {
    ID          string      // matches beads epic ID
    RigName     string
    EpicID      string      // beads epic tracking this swarm
    BaseCommit  string      // git SHA all workers branch from
    Integration string      // integration branch name
    State       SwarmState
    CreatedAt   time.Time
    Workers     []string    // polecat names assigned
}

// SwarmTask represents a single task in the swarm (maps to beads issue)
type SwarmTask struct {
    IssueID   string  // beads issue ID
    Assignee  string  // polecat name
    Branch    string  // worker branch name
    State     string  // mirrors beads status
}
```

## Note

Swarm state is primarily stored IN beads. These types are in-memory representations for the SwarmManager to work with. No separate manifest.json files.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:08:30.364047-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T13:31:56.20815-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T13:31:56.20815-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-kmn.1","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:08:30.364431-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-kmn.1","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:20.646487-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-kmn.10","title":"Batch work landing report generation","description":"Generate reports on batch work completion.

## Trigger

When all children of an epic are closed, generate a completion report.

## Report Contents

- Epic metadata (ID, title, created by, duration)
- Task summary (completed counts, timing)
- Per-task details (issue, assignee, time taken)
- Git stats (commits merged, lines changed)
- Cleanup stats (branches deleted)

## Output Locations

1. Mail to Mayor - summary report
2. Optional: save to file with --save flag

## Command

```bash
gt report --epic <epic-id> [--save report.md]
```

## Format

Markdown with sections:
- Summary
- Tasks
- Workers
- Timeline

## Note

This replaces the swarm-based reporting (gt-662). Reports are generated from epic/issue data, not from separate swarm state files.

## Reference

PGT: swarm/report.py (for format ideas, ignore swarm ID patterns)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:10:23.242931-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:26:27.99208-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-kmn.10","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:10:23.243287-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-o40t","title":"gt sling --force: return displaced work to ready pool","description":"When slinging with --force to an agent with occupied hook, the displaced molecule should be returned to the ready pool rather than silently orphaned.

Current behavior:
- --force overwrites the hook attachment
- Previous work becomes orphaned (still assigned but not pinned)

Desired behavior:
1. Unpin the displaced molecule (clear assignee, set pinned=false)
2. Print warning in tool output: 'Warning: displaced gt-xxx back to ready pool'
3. Proceed with new sling

This ensures:
- No silent data loss
- Agent sees the warning and can act on it
- Human caller sees what happened
- Displaced work is discoverable via 'bd ready'

Implementation location: checkHookCollision() or the sling handlers themselves (after the --force check passes)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T16:00:23.713733-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T16:07:14.482319-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T16:07:14.482319-08:00","close_reason":"Closed"}

Reproduction:

  ● 🤝 HANDOFF: Swarm session complete...

 {"id":"gt-odvf","title":"Document bd mol bond/squash/burn CLI","description":"Create CLI reference documentation for molecule commands:

## bd mol bond

Instantiate a proto into a runnable molecule.

```bash
bd mol bond <proto-id> [--wisp] [--assignee=<addr>]
```

- Default: creates a Mol (durable, in main beads)
- --wisp: creates a Wisp (ephemeral, in .beads-ephemeral/)
- --assignee: who will execute this molecule

## bd mol squash

Complete a molecule and generate digest.

```bash
bd mol squash <mol-id> --summary='...'
```

- For Mol: creates digest in git history
- For Wisp: evaporates (no permanent record)
- --summary: required summary of what was accomplished

## bd mol burn

Abandon a molecule without completing.

```bash
bd mol burn <mol-id> [--reason='...']
```

- Discards molecule state
- No digest created

 {"id":"gt-ogpk","title":"Add neighbor-check steps to all patrol molecules","description":"Part of the 'Gas Town is a Village' antifragility design.

Every patrol molecule should include optional neighbor-checking:
- Deacon checks Witness and Refinery health
- Witness checks Refinery health  
- Refinery checks Witness health
- Polecats can peek other polecats

Use gt peek to check health states.
If stuck neighbor found, can nudge or escalate.

This creates distributed monitoring - no single point of failure.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:01:16.358314-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:01:16.358314-08:00"}
